# With merge restack method, repo sync should delete merged branches
# while marking upstack branches as needs restack (without rebasing them).

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-04-17T09:10:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# Configure merge restacking.
git config spice.restack.method merge

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub register alice
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create and submit a stack: main -> feat1 -> feat2
git add feat1.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feat1' feat1
git add feat2.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feat2' feat2
gs stack submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'

# Merge the first change and sync.
shamhub merge alice/example 1
gs repo sync
stderr 'feat1: #1 was merged'
stderr 'feat2: moved upstack onto main \(needs restack\)'
stderr 'feat1: deleted \(was [a-f0-9]+\)'

# feat1 should be deleted, feat2 should remain and need restack.
! git rev-parse --verify feat1
git rev-parse --verify feat2

gs ll -a
stderr 'feat2 \(#2\) \(needs restack\)'

-- repo/feat1.txt --
feature 1

-- repo/feat2.txt --
feature 2
